steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Recall the triangle area formula
steps.content:
The area of a triangle is given by:
$$A = \frac{1}{2} \times \text{base} \times \text{height}$$
We are given base = 4 inches and height = 17 inches.

steps.title: Substitute and calculate
steps.content:
Substitute the values into the formula:
$$A = \frac{1}{2} \times 4 \times 17$$
Simplify:
$$A = 2 \times 17 = 34$$
That is the correct answer.
